entries.content: First day back from break. Set up DailyDispatch project repo and started scaffolding the HTML structure. Going with single-file approach — everything inline. #planning
Standup: @Dave working on IndexedDB layer, @Sarah reviewing PRD draft. Blocked on font licensing for Inter. #standup
Decided to use Inter + JetBrains Mono — both open source, no licensing issues. Ship it. #decision
Product review with @Kate and @Sarah. Agreed on core loop: capture → feed → search. Tasks come in v2. #meeting #decision

# Q1 Retrospective

## What went well
- Shipped v1.0 with feed, search, sections
- Single-file architecture holding up
- @Dave's IndexedDB layer is solid

## What didn't
- Took too long on theme system (scope creep)
- Floating note had 3 failed approaches before localStorage solution
- No automated testing

## Action items
- {{task:t_001}}
- Start writing regression test plan
- @Sarah to own release process going forward
Standup: Feed rendering complete, working on tag extraction and #tag pill rendering. @Alex starting on search. #standup
Met with @Lisa and @Priya about the visual design system. Going with warm Notion-inspired palette, dense UI, no rounded pills. #meeting #design

# v2.0 Feature Spec

## Goals
- Add task management (inline in notes)
- Floating note window for meetings
- Smart Lists for embedded search

## Non-goals
- Mobile app (desktop first)
- Cloud sync (local only)
- Plugin system

## Success metrics
- User can create a task in < 3 seconds
- Floating note data survives window close 100% of the time
- Smart List renders < 200ms with 500 entries

## Timeline
- Jan: spec + design
- Feb: build
- Mar: launch v3.0

Reviewed with @Kate and @Mike. #roadmap #planning
Standup: Search working with #tags, @mentions, keywords, OR groups. @Alex crushed it. #standup
Decision: sections will be saved filter queries, not folders. This is the key architectural insight — everything is a view, nothing is a container. #decision
Standup: Sidebar sections live. Drag to reorder working. @Dave fixed the IDB race condition on save. #standup
Design review with @Lisa. Chose warm amber as accent color, not blue. Feels more personal for a journal app. #meeting #design #decision
Standup: Starting on task system. Decided on {{task:id}} token approach — tasks live in the note content as tokens, rendered as widgets. #standup #decision

# Floating Note Architecture

## Problem
Users on video calls need a quick note window that doesn't cover the call.

## Solution
- `window.open()` creates popup
- localStorage is source of truth (sync is synchronous)
- Main window polls every 1s for close signal
- On close: localStorage write → main window recovers → IDB persist

## Why not IDB directly?
IDB writes are async. `beforeunload` is sync. The writes get dropped on window close. localStorage is the only reliable path.

## Tested with
- @Lisa: real meeting note flow
- @Priya: edge cases (crash, reload, multiple windows)

#design #ux
Shipped v1.5 with sections, tag editor, people list, and 5 themes. @Sarah handled the release. #shipped
Sprint planning: 2-week sprint for task system. @Dave on IDB schema, @Alex on CM6 widget, me on task modal. #meeting #sprint
Valentine's Day but we're shipping. Task creation working end to end — /task in note → modal → board. @Alex demo'd it. #shipped
Standup: Kanban drag-and-drop between columns working. @Dave fixed the order persistence bug. List view toggle done. #standup
Retro: task sprint went well. Velocity 42 points. Floating note had 3 failed approaches (IDB race, postMessage, BroadcastChannel) before localStorage. #retro
User feedback from @Tom (beta tester): 'I actually use this every day now. The floating note during standup is a game changer.' #feedback
Sprint planning: Smart Lists, daily summary, onboarding checklist. 2 weeks. @Alex on Smart Lists, @Priya on summary dashboard. #meeting #sprint
Smart List prototype working — {{smartlist:#meeting}} renders live results inline in the note. @Alex shipped it in 2 days. #shipped
Standup: Onboarding checklist 80% done. 8 steps, auto-detection. @Priya making each step clickable with pulse highlight. #standup

# v3.0 Launch Plan

## What's shipping
- Task management (create, edit, complete)
- Board view with drag-and-drop
- List view with grouping
- Floating note window
- Smart Lists
- Daily summary dashboard
- 15 themes (10 light, 5 dark)
- Onboarding checklist
- Getting started tour

## Launch checklist
- [x] All features smoke tested
- [x] Help pages written (13 pages)
- [x] Homepage updated with screenshots
- [x] Export/import tested with merge mode
- [ ] Product Hunt listing prepared
- [ ] Demo video recorded

## Team
- @Sarah: release management
- @Kate: marketing copy
- @Dave: infrastructure
- @Alex: feature dev
- @Lisa: visual design
- @Priya: UX + onboarding

#launch #roadmap
v3.0 IS LIVE. Single HTML file, no dependencies, no account needed. @Sarah cut the release at 2pm. The team is incredible. #shipped #launch
First weekend after launch. 47 downloads, 12 GitHub stars. Not viral but the people using it love it. @Tom says he's migrated fully from Bear. #metrics #feedback
Standup: Post-launch bug fixes. @Alex fixing CM6 task widget spacing, @Dave on IDB error handling. I'm on theme polish. #standup
Design review with @Lisa: 15 themes finalized. Hot Pink as default (bold choice but it's our brand). Nordic and Midnight are the dark favorites. #meeting #design
Decision: rename Kanban to Board in all user-facing text. Keep kanban in code. Users don't know what Kanban means. #decision #ux
Sprint planning: Calendar view, query extensions (date:, completed:), homepage overhaul. @Alex leading calendar, @Priya on personas. #meeting #sprint
Daily summary dashboard shipped! Metrics cards, activity timeline, task completion tracking. Looks world-class. @Priya's design is stunning. #shipped
Added date: and completed: query operators. Smart Lists can now do completed:today to show tasks done today. Game changer for daily reviews. #shipped
Standup: @Alex finished calendar view for tasks. Monthly grid, task pills, + button per day. Looks clean. Hiding filter bar in calendar mode. #standup
User research call with @Jordan (power user, consultant). Key insight: they use sections as project dashboards with Smart Lists. Want better export. #meeting #feedback #research
Typographic scale shipped: --fs-xs through --fs-2xl replacing 155 hardcoded font sizes. Future user font preference now trivial to add. #shipped #infrastructure
Met with @Kate about Product Hunt launch strategy. She wants to position us as 'the anti-Notion' — local-first, no account, single file. I love it. #meeting #launch

# Product Hunt Launch Plan

## Positioning
**Tagline:** Your work journal in a single HTML file. No account. No cloud. No compromises.

## Key differentiators
1. Single file — open in any browser, inspect every line
2. No account — zero signup, zero data collection
3. Tasks + notes together — not two separate apps
4. Floating note window — take notes during video calls

## Launch day plan
- Ship at 12:01 AM PST
- @Kate posts maker comment at 8 AM
- @Sarah monitors feedback + responds
- I do 3 Twitter threads throughout the day

## Assets needed
- {{task:t_012}}
- {{task:t_011}}
- 5 screenshots showing key features
- GIF of floating note in action

#launch #roadmap
Standup: Homepage CSS art screenshot done — shows full app layout. Persona cards added (Engineer, PM, Researcher, Founder). @Priya reviewed. #standup
Blocker: search input in tasks view loses focus after one character. renderActiveView() rebuilds DOM and kills the input. Need to restore focus+cursor after re-render. #blocker
Fixed: search input now restores focus and cursor position after re-render. Simple but was driving people crazy. #shipped
Morning standup: @Alex finishing calendar view polish, @Dave reviewing PR for settings schema unification, @Priya updating help pages. #standup
Section live dots shipped — green pulsing dot next to section name when filter has results. Small touch but makes sections feel alive. #shipped #ux
Merged @Alex's PR: unified .btn-primary class, buildSettingsObject(), Download .md button. Good cleanup batch. #shipped

# Q2 Sprint Themes

## Theme 1: Launch Polish (Apr 14-25)
- Product Hunt launch materials
- Demo video
- Landing page final pass
- Competitive analysis

## Theme 2: Onboarding (Apr 28 - May 9)
- Migration guide from Obsidian
- Interactive tutorial improvements
- Keyboard shortcut overlay

## Theme 3: Performance (May 12-23)
- File System Access API for auto-backup
- Lazy loading for large journals (1000+ entries)
- CM6 decoration cache

## Theme 4: Mobile Exploration (Jun)
- Read-only mobile view mockups
- PWA investigation
- Touch-friendly interactions

@Sarah @Dave @Kate — let's discuss priorities Monday.

#planning #roadmap
Realized we need mock data for testing import/merge. Building a full PM dataset from Jan to today — meetings, decisions, sprints, the whole thing. #idea
